objections.claim: The functional oracle is an open assertion: saying the suite covers “EVERY interactive element” does not establish a closed inventory or prove test coverage equals that inventory. A suite can pass while an unregistered control, reachable machine transition, export, or canvas-only interaction remains untested. The candidate also leaves the executable runner unresolved as “Playwright / chrome-devtools MCP.”

The Evidence Ledger mechanism is insufficiently fail-closed and violates the content-identity discipline. “Exact record/commit” does not require full 40-hex identities, directly reading broad institutional-memory directories risks publishing unintended material, and content addressing does not make information impossible to lose. The plan also does not explicitly preserve the spine as read-only.

The accessibility and reduced-motion claims are unsound: invisible semantic controls do not provide accessible canvas content, meaningful focus presentation, logical reading order, or a reduced-motion/no-WebGL equivalent “for free.” Axe alone cannot certify those properties.

The performance gate would not discriminate because “loads + runs in seconds” supplies no numeric budgets, pinned browser/profile, repetition rule, or failure treatment. The listed missing browser-driver and 3D-walkthrough capabilities are not resolved before acceptance depends on them.
